data.title: Grain Traders Focused On Russian-Ukraine War, Weather — Market Talk
data.description: Tentative steps to end the Russia-Ukraine war, as well as cold weather afflicting the Black Sea area wheat crop, are both garnering the attention of grain traders. "Wheat is… finding support from reductions to the Russian crop and building concerns over the Indian crop," says Karl Setzer of Consus Ag Consulting. He adds that in addition to a supply squeeze coming for Russian crops, world export demand is also a key focus. "India is already seeing its wheat supplies drop and now another short crop will likely lead to imports," says Setzer. Wheat is up 3.9% this afternoon, to $6 a bushel- the first time they've been that high since October. Corn is up 0.6% and soybeans rise 0.8%. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

data.title: DraftKings Shares at 3-Year High After Super Bowl, Hot Start to 2025
data.description:
By Dean Seal

Shares of DraftKings are trading at a three-year high after the company raised its revenue outlook for the year and said it has been making more money on player bets.

The Boston-based sports-betting platform said after Thursday's closing bell that it expects more revenue than originally anticipated in 2025 as the company leans into live betting.

DraftKings also reported that its hold rate, or the cut of wagered money the sportsbook keeps after paying out winners, was 11% in January and 13% so far in February, including a blockbuster performance during the Super Bowl. That's up from 9.4% in 2024. "We're off to an excellent start," Chief Financial Officer Alan Ellingson said on a call with analysts.

The stock rose 15% to a high of $53.47 early in Friday's session. Shares haven't traded this high since September 2021.

The gains came even though fourth-quarter earnings and revenue were shy of analyst estimates. Sportsbooks had a rough run in the last three months of 2024 as more wins by favorites and fewer underdog upsets ate into their hold rates and forced them to pay out more winnings.

There was some concern that those lower 2024 hold rates would prompt a cut to DraftKings's 2025 guidance, but that didn't happen, Mizuho analysts said in a research note.

"Conversely, it sounds like DraftKings is having better than expected success with structural hold," the analysts said.

DraftKings is now guiding for $6.3 billion to $6.6 billion in revenue this year, a $50 million lift at the midpoint from its previous guidance.

The new outlook doesn't factor in this year's Super Bowl, during which DraftKings saw a record $436 million in bets placed and generated more gross gaming revenue than on any other day in the company's history. The DraftKings sportsbook app reached the top spot in the sports category of Apple's App Store during the Super Bowl and the No. 3 spot across all apps, Ellingson said.

The guidance raise does contemplate enhancements DraftKings has made to its live-betting offering, according to an investor slide deck.

The company reached a deal in August to acquire Simplebet, which provides in-game wager-pricing information for sports leagues, with the goal of improving DraftKing's live-betting capabilities. The addition should increase DraftKings's hold rate and the mix of parlay bets it takes, Benchmark analyst Mike Hickey said in a research note on Friday.

Write to Dean Seal at dean.seal@wsj.com

data.title: AtriCure, Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
AtriCure, Inc., a leading innovator in treatments for atrial fibrillation (Afib), left atrial appendage (LAA) management, and post-operative pain management, has released its 2024 10-K report. The report highlights significant financial growth, strategic initiatives, and the challenges faced by the company in a competitive and evolving market.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenue: $465.3 million, increased by 16.5% from the previous year, driven by growth in all key product lines and new product launches.

Gross Profit: $347.5 million, representing a gross margin of 74.7%, slightly down from 75.2% in the prior year due to less favorable geographic and product mix.

Loss from Operations: $(40.0) million, reflecting an increase in operating expenses, particularly in research and development and selling, general and administrative expenses.

Net Loss: $(44.7) million, compared to a net loss of $(30.4) million in the previous year, impacted by increased operating expenses and a loss on debt extinguishment.

Basic and Diluted Net Loss Per Share: $(0.95), compared to $(0.66) in the prior year, reflecting the increased net loss.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The company reported growth across all key product lines, with open ablation revenue increasing by 17.4%, minimally invasive ablation by 2.6%, pain management by 25.7%, and appendage management by 12.7% in the United States.

Geographical Performance: International revenue saw a significant increase of 25.6%, driven by growth across all franchises and major geographic regions.

New Product Launches: In 2024, the company launched several new products, including the EnCompass clamp in CE-marked countries, the EPi-Ease Hybrid access device, the EnCapture clamp, the cryoSPHERE+ cryoablation probe, the cryoSPHERE MAX probe, and the AtriClip FLEX-Mini device in the United States.

New Production Initiatives: The company expanded its manufacturing and engineering facilities in Mason, Ohio, to support growth and better serve customers.

Future Outlook: The company plans to continue investing in product innovation, clinical science, and physician education to expand awareness and adoption of its products. It also anticipates further international expansion and regulatory approvals to drive growth.

Strategic Initiatives

Strategic Initiatives: In 2024, the company focused on product innovation, clinical science, and physician education to drive growth. Key initiatives included the launch of new products such as the EnCompass clamp in Europe, the EPi-Ease Hybrid access device, and the cryoSPHERE+ cryoablation probe in the U.S. The company also entered into an exclusive licensing agreement to co-develop pulsed field ablation technology, reflecting its commitment to expanding its product portfolio and market reach.

Capital Management: The company entered into a new asset-based credit agreement providing a $125 million revolving credit facility, with an option to increase by an additional $40 million. This facility was used to refinance existing debt, resulting in a loss on debt extinguishment of $1.362 million. The company also maintained a strong cash position with $122.721 million in cash and cash equivalents as of December 31, 2024. Share-based compensation expenses were recognized, and the company continued to issue common stock under equity incentive plans and an employee stock purchase plan.

Future Outlook: The company plans to continue investing in product development and regulatory approvals to support its growth strategy. It anticipates further capital expenditures to expand manufacturing capabilities and enhance operational efficiency. The company will also focus on maintaining financial flexibility by monitoring macroeconomic conditions and exploring additional financing options if necessary. The strategic initiatives are expected to drive revenue growth and improve market penetration in the coming years.

Challenges and Risks

Commercial Execution and Product Performance Risks: Failure to achieve widespread market acceptance domestically may harm operating results. Competition from existing and new products and procedures may decrease market share. Clinical data may be negative, or trials may not satisfy regulatory requirements, slowing adoption.

Industry Condition Risks: A prolonged downturn in macroeconomic conditions may materially adversely affect the business. Government and private payors may contain or reduce healthcare spending, impacting reimbursement for procedures using the company's products.

Operational Risks: Unfavorable publicity relating to the business or industry could negatively impact operations or stock price. Reliance on single and limited source third-party suppliers could harm the business if they cannot provide materials or services timely.

Legal & Compliance Risks: The company could face substantial penalties if it does not fully comply with federal, state, and foreign regulations. Modifications to products may require new clearances or approvals by FDA, and failure to obtain such clearances could result in recalls.

Financial Risks: The company has a history of net losses and may never become profitable. Quarterly financial results are likely to fluctuate significantly due to uncertain adoption rates of products by clinicians.

Common Stock Risks: The company may fail to achieve publicly announced guidance, causing a decline in stock price. Securities analysts may discontinue coverage, negatively impacting the market price of common stock.

Management’s Discussion and Analysis: Management has identified increased competition and regulatory challenges as primary risks. The company is focusing on expanding its product offerings and increasing market penetration to mitigate these risks. Efforts are being made to enhance the company's competitive position by investing in research and development and pursuing strategic partnerships.

Quantitative and Qualitative Disclosures About Market Risk: The company is exposed to foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations, which may adversely affect its financial results. The majority of the company's transactions are in U.S. Dollars, but direct sales outside the United States are transacted in Euros, British Pounds, Canadian Dollars, or Australian Dollars, exposing the company to currency risk.

SEC Filing: AtriCure, Inc. [ ATRC ] - 10-K - Feb. 14, 2025

data.title: Sector Update: Financial Stocks Advance Friday Afternoon
data.description:
Financial stocks were rising in Friday afternoon trading, with the NYSE Financial Index up 0.3% and the Financial Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLF) adding 0.4%.

The Philadelphia Housing Index was rising 0.5%, and the Real Estate Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLRE) was 0.2% higher.

Bitcoin (BTC/USD) was increasing 1.8% to $98,373, and the yield for 10-year US Treasuries fell 6 basis points to 4.47%.

In economic news, US retail sales fell by 0.9% in January, versus the 0.2% decrease expected in a survey compiled by Bloomberg and the previous month's 0.7% gain.

President Donald Trump ordered officials to develop custom tariffs for imports from all US trading partners and report their recommendations for imposing reciprocal tariffs.

In corporate news, Blackstone is mulling a sale of Olympus Energy, its natural gas exploration company, that could be valued at up to $2 billion, Bloomberg reported. Blackstone shares rose 2.7%.

Brookfield Asset Management has closed its Brookfield Growth venture capital-focused unit, Bloomberg. Brookfield Asset shares added 3.3%.

HSBC is reportedly set to unveil $1.5 billion in annual cost savings as part of Chief Executive Georges Elhedery's restructuring efforts, the Financial Times reported. HSBC shares were up 0.1%.

data.title: Sector Update: Energy Stocks Rise in Friday Afternoon Trading
data.description:
Energy stocks were higher Friday afternoon, with the NYSE Energy Sector Index up 0.6% and the Energy Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLE) adding 0.8%.

The Philadelphia Oil Service Sector index dropped 0.9%, and the Dow Jones US Utilities index eased 0.1%.

Front-month West Texas Intermediate crude oil was declining 1% to $70.57 a barrel while the global benchmark Brent crude contract shed 0.6% to $74.58 a barrel. Henry Hub natural gas futures rose 3% to $3.74 per 1 million BTU.

In corporate news, Diamondback Energy is in talks to buy Double Eagle in a deal potentially valuing the business at over $5 billion, The Wall Street Journal reported. Diamondback shares were down fractionally.

Enbridge Chief Executive Greg Ebel said Friday on Bloomberg TV that the scrapped Northern Gateway oil pipeline proposal would be difficult to revive without major legislative changes in Canada. Enbridge shares were falling 3.7%.

Epsilon Energy shares popped nearly 4% after the company said late Thursday its board approved a new one-year program under which the company may repurchase up to 2.2 million shares for an aggregate purchase price of not more than $13 million.

data.title: World Acceptance Corp Officer Sells Shares
data.description:
Reporter NameChilders Jason E.
RelationshipSVP, Information Technology
TypeSell
Amount$186,911
SEC FilingForm 4

Jason E. Childers, SVP of Information Technology at World Acceptance Corp, sold 1,259 shares of common stock at a weighted average price of $148.46 per share, totaling $186,911. Following the transaction, Childers directly owns 6,780 shares of the company's common stock.

SEC Filing: WORLD ACCEPTANCE CORP [ WRLD ] - Form 4 - Feb. 14, 2025

data.title: Toyota Motor Develops New Hydrogen Fuel Cell System
data.description:
Toyota Motor said Friday it has developed a third-generation hydrogen fuel cell system that offers the same durability as diesel-powered engines.

Compared with the previous version, the new fuel cell system is up to two times more durable and 1.2 times more fuel efficient, the automaker said, adding that the improved fuel efficiency allows for 20% additional cruising range.

The new system, which will be expanded for use in heavy-duty commercial vehicles, is expected to be launched after 2026 at the earliest in markets mainly in China, Europe, Japan, and North America, Toyota said.

data.title: Bank Lending Is Sensitive to Fed Policy — Market Talk
data.description: After the financial crisis, years of a zero interest-rate benchmark from the Federal Reserve didn't do as much as some economists had expected it would to stimulate financial activity and growth. That led some economists to question whether the Fed's ability to spur bank lending had weakened. Mark Spiegel of the San Francisco Fed finds in new research that this "bank-lending channel" of Fed policy action has come back, however. Studying data from after the Fed's first post-GFC rate hike in 2016, Spiegel finds that banks' lending activity is once again responsive to monetary-policy shocks. Large banks remain a bit less sensitive to Fed policy than small and medium banks, he finds. (matt.grossman@wsj.com, @mattgrossman)

data.title: Cyprus and Chevron-led group agree on updated plan for offshore field
data.description:
Cyprus and a Chevron-led consortium have agreed on an updated development plan over an offshore gas field off Cyprus, a joint statement by the two sides said on Friday.

There had been protracted talks on the future development of the Aphrodite gas field south-east of Cyprus since Chevron attempted to introduce changes to a 2019 field development plan. That plan had been agreed between Cyprus and Noble, the former licence-holder and an independent energy operator Chevron acquired in 2020.

The approved Development and Production Plan (DPP) announced on Friday includes a floating production unit, also known as a gas processing facility, and a pipeline for exporting gas to Egypt.

"We look forward to the expeditious development of the field via Egypt's facilities, a tangible step towards establishing the role that Cyprus envisions in the energy sector of the region, for the benefit of the country and its people," the joint statement quoted Cypriot Energy Minister George Papanastasiou as saying.

Frank Cassulo, Vice President at Chevron International Exploration and Production said it was a "major development".

"Chevron values its partnership with the Republic of Cyprus and we believe it is important that Aphrodite is developed for the benefit of the Republic of Cyprus, and the Eastern Mediterranean region.

"This is also aligned with our strategy to deliver affordable, reliable, and ever-cleaner energy."

The approval of the modifications to the DPP and the Production Sharing Contract milestones aim to advance technical and engineering work in relation to Front End Engineering and Design and towards a Final Investment Decision for Aphrodite, the two parties said.

Aphrodite was discovered in 2011 and is Cyprus's first offshore discovery, holding an estimated 3.5 trillion cubic feet (tcf) of gas. Cyprus has since discovered gas deposits in another four areas, all untapped to date.

Chevron is a partner in the field with Israel's NewMed Energy (NWNDpTA) and BG Cyprus Ltd., part of Shell .

data.title: Sector Update: Health Care
data.description:
Health care stocks declined Friday afternoon, with the NYSE Health Care Index falling 0.7% and the Health Care Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLV) down 0.5%.

The iShares Biotechnology ETF (IBB) eased 0.1%.

In corporate news, Moderna reported a Q4 loss of $2.91 per share, worse than the $2.83 loss expected in a FactSet poll. Revenue was $966 million, better than the consensus estimate of $942.8 million. Its shares rose 3%.

data.title: Newmark up after upbeat annual forecasts, quarterly results
data.description:
** Shares of commercial real estate services company Newmark up as much as 12.3% to $15.52

** Company sees 2025 revenue between $2.9 billion and $3.1 billion compared to $2.75 billion in 2024

** Also sees annual adjusted EPS between $1.40 and $1.50 vs $1.23 in 2024

** Reports fourth-quarter revenue rise of $888.3 million and adjusted EPS of 55 cents compared to 46 cents a year ago

** Says revenue growth from strong performance accross segments including Management Services, Leasing and Other Commissions, and Capital Markets

** NMRK rose ~16.9% in 2024

data.title: Meta's $65 Billion Power Move: AI Humanoid Robots That Could Take Over Your Home
data.description:
Meta Platforms is making a serious push into AI-powered humanoid robotics, setting the stage for a new frontier in tech. The company is assembling a specialized team inside its Reality Labs division, led by Marc Whitten, to develop the AI, sensors, and software that could power the next wave of humanoid robots. Unlike Tesla , which is manufacturing its own Optimus robot, Meta's initial play is to become the backbone of the industrysimilar to what Google's Android did for smartphones. The company has already started talks with robotics firms like Unitree Robotics and Figure AI. With plans to hire 100 engineers this year and billions committed to AI and AR/VR, Meta is placing a major bet on humanoid robots as the next leap in smart home technology.

Meta's move comes as humanoid robotics remains an unsolved puzzlecurrent models struggle with even basic tasks like folding clothes or carrying a glass of water. But Meta believes its advances in AI and AR/VR data collection could accelerate development. Big Tech is already circling this spaceApple (AAPL) and Google DeepMind have ramped up robotics research, sensing the same opportunity. While widespread adoption is still years away, Meta's playbook suggests it wants to be at the center of this transformation.

Financially, this is a high-stakes game. Meta plans to spend $65 billion this year on AI infrastructure, which includes this new robotics push. Meanwhile, Tesla has started limited production of Optimus, with a target consumer price of $30,000. Other players, like Boston Dynamics, are already selling robotic systems for warehouse automation, but Meta has its sights on something biggerturning humanoid robots into household essentials. If this gamble pays off, Meta won't just be a social media giant anymore; it will be a key player in shaping AI-powered robotics. For investors, this could be the start of one of the biggest technological shifts of the decade, with Meta is trying to position itself as the platform that powers it all.

data.title: French Regulators Remove Bybit from Blacklist, Crypto Exchange Now Targets MiCA License
data.description:
Bybit, one of the renowned cryptocurrency exchanges, was removed from the regulatory blacklist of France’s Autorité des Marchés Financiers (AMF).

“After more than 2 years of working with the French regulator through multiple remediation efforts, BYBIT is now officially removed from France AMF blacklist. MiCA license next,” Ben Zhou, the Co-founder and CEO of BYBIT, said on X.

From Blacklist to Compliance

After two years of remediation efforts and negotiations, the exchange secured approval from the French watchdog. Now, Bybit is shifting focus toward obtaining a Markets in Crypto-Assets (MiCA) license, a key regulatory certification that would enable it to operate legally across the European Economic Area (EEA).

Bybit found itself on the AMF blacklist in May 2022 for reportedly failing to comply with local financial regulations. At the time, French authorities warned investors against using the platform, urging them to shift to regulated exchanges.

Ben Zhou
@benbybit

After more than 2 years of working with the French regulator through multiple remediation efforts, BYBIT is now officially removed from France AMF blacklist. MiCA license next. pic.twitter.com/irPf5bOSBp

Feb 14, 2025

In response, Bybit ceased offering services to French residents last year while working behind the scenes to address regulatory concerns. After an extended dialogue with the AMF and multiple compliance adjustments, Bybit CEO Ben Zhou announced on X that the exchange was officially removed from the blacklist.

MiCA License for Full EU Market Access

With the French regulatory hurdle cleared, Bybit has now set its sights on acquiring a MiCA license. The MiCA framework, introduced by the European Union, establishes a uniform regulatory structure for crypto firms, allowing licensed platforms to operate freely across all 30 EEA nations.

While Bybit has made progress in France, it still faces regulatory challenges in other key markets. In Malaysia, the Securities Commission ordered Bybit to halt operations, citing non-compliance.

Similarly, in India, the exchange suspended services following regulatory scrutiny, despite later securing a local license after paying a $1 million fine.

Other crypto firms are also pushing for MiCA approval, seeing it as a gateway to expand in Europe. Competitors like Bitget, OKX, and Crypto.com have all pursued or secured MiCA licenses to gain a foothold in the region.

data.title: Corn, soybean bids flat to higher with steady farmer selling
data.description:
Spot basis bids for corn and soybeans were steady to higher at elevators, processors and river terminals around the Midwest in a spot check on Friday, with dealers saying farmers were selling as futures rose.

* Basis bids for soybeans rose at processors in Decatur and Morristown, Indiana, an elevator in Blair, Nebraska and a river terminal in Seneca, Illinois.

* Corn basis bids improved at a processor in Cedar Rapids, Iowa and the elevator in Blair, Nebraska, as well as the river terminal in Seneca.

* CBOT March soybeans (SH25) were up 6-3/4 cents at $10.36-3/4 a bushel at 12:30 p.m. CST (1830 GMT).

* CBOT March corn futures were up 2-1/4 cents at $4.95-3/4 per bushel.

data.title: Waste Connections Target Lifted to US$210, at Truist Securities
data.description:
Truist Securities has raised its target on Waste Connections to US$210, from US$200 and reiterated its Buy rating after the company reported mixed fourth-quarter results and issued 2025 guidance in-line with consensus.

Truist expects another year of "outsized" margin expansion to be driven by favorable price-cost spread with core pricing expected to be ~6%.

"We see improving FCF conversion rates over the next several years driven by core margin expansion and sustainability investments coming online in 2026/2027. We believe the set-up for the sector remains favorable and see WCN's superior business quality supporting a premium valuation to peers."

data.title: Dell Nears $5B Server Deal for Musk's xAI, Bloomberg Reports
data.description:
• Dell Technologies is closing in on a $5 billion deal to provide Elon Musk's xAI with servers, Bloomberg reports, citing unnamed sources.
  • Dell will sell servers containing Nvidia's GB200 semiconductors to the AI startup for delivery this year, according to the report.
  • Some details are being finalized and may still change, the sources told Bloomberg.
  • Dell and Nvidia declined Bloomberg's request for comment, while xAI didn't respond to a request for comment.

Full story: https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2025-02-14/dell-nears-deal-with-musk-s-xai-for-5-billion-in-servers-with-nvidia-chips

Write to Dean Seal at dean.seal@wsj.com

data.title: Bank Lending Is Sensitive to Fed Policy — Market Talk
data.description:
1337 ET - After the financial crisis, years of a zero interest-rate benchmark from the Federal Reserve didn't do as much as some economists had expected it would to stimulate financial activity and growth. That led some economists to question whether the Fed's ability to spur bank lending had weakened. Mark Spiegel of the San Francisco Fed finds in new research that this "bank-lending channel" of Fed policy action has come back, however. Studying data from after the Fed's first post-GFC rate hike in 2016, Spiegel finds that banks' lending activity is once again responsive to monetary-policy shocks. Large banks remain a bit less sensitive to Fed policy than small and medium banks, he finds. (matt.grossman@wsj.com, @mattgrossman)

1334 ET - The number of rigs drilling for oil in the U.S. edged up by one this week to 481, which was 16 fewer than a year ago, oil services company Baker Hughes reports, while natural gas rigs increased by one to 101, down 20 from a year before. In the Dallas Fed's 4Q energy survey published last month, two-thirds of exploration and production executives who anticipate increasing investment this year beyond their previous estimate said they expect to invest more in drilling and completion of wells. A majority of executives also expect permitting times for drilling wells on federal lands to decrease over the next four years. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1310 ET - Some of the most-followed real-time estimates of 1Q GDP growth move lower after the disappointing January retail-sales data--but forecasters are still calling for a moderate economic expansion in the Jan.-to-March quarter. The economics team at Goldman Sachs lowers its 1Q GDP growth forecast to a 2% annualized rate, from 2.3% previously. The GDPNow model hosted by the Atlanta Fed now sees the economy growing at a 2.3% clip this quarter, down from 2.9% in the previous estimate. The New York Fed's version of a GDP-tracker model now sees a 3% GDP growth rate in 1Q, a slight decline from 3.1% a week ago. The Commerce Department's advance estimate shows that last quarter, the economy grew at a 2.3% annualized rate. (matt.grossman@wsj.com, @mattgrossman)

1252 ET - Republic Services doesn't expect its operations to get hit by the economic implications of Trump tariff threats, at least for now. While the waste management company is mindful of the macro environment, CEO Jon Vander Ark says on a call with analysts, what happens with tariffs and the impact on inflation doesn't keep him up at night, he says. "We've lived through a pandemic and high inflation and war at the shore of Europe and other elements," Vander Ark says. Regarding Trump's approach to green energy, Republic says it didn't include the Department of Energy's compressed natural gas tax credits in its guidance for 2025. Shares rise 2.75 to $230.50. (sabela.ojea@wsj.com; @sabelaojeaguix)

1224 ET - Republic Services' pricing is coming down as its cost inflation drops, CEO Jon Vander Ark says on a call with analysts. The waste management company's retention rate remains very healthy as it gives customers a price point that fosters loyalty, Vander Ark says. But one area the company isn't pleased with is the municipal space. "That's one on the restricted side, and that's just an area of the business in general where customers on balance are not paying their fair share," he says. "We put an enormous amount of innovation, capital and labor into that equation, and we have many attractive customers and contracts, but not all of them meet our standard," the executive says. "You'll continue to see us optimize that portfolio over the next few years." Shares rise 2.7% to $230.61. (sabela.ojea@wsj.com; @sabelaojeaguix)

1201 ET - Republic Services is accelerating its M&A investments and expects to deploy at least $1 billion toward acquisitions in 2025, CEO Jon Vander Ark says on a call with analysts. For the past few years, the waste-management company has invested around $500 million in these deals, Vander Ark says. "The outsized number this year is predicated off a really strong start already, so we feel really good about the momentum the team has, and things that are either closed or nearly closed on that front." The company expects most of its M&A agreements to come from environmental services in the first half, and the back half of the year to be more heavily slanted toward recycling and waste, the executive adds. The company's guidance for 2025 includes the financial contribution from four acquisitions closed to-date. Shares rise 3.1%, to $231.45. (sabela.ojea@wsj.com; @sabelaojeaguix)

1156 ET - Republic Services expects to benefit from its new recycling center, located in Indiana, in the second half of 2025, CEO Jon Vander Ark says on a call with analysts. The waste management company has completed construction at the Indiana Polymer Center and equipment commission is underway, Vander Ark says. Overall for next year, the company is looking at incremental revenue of around $70 million, and incremental earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization of $35 million across its portfolio of sustainability investments. The company also expects to spend another $75 million on Polymer Centers this year. Regarding its renewable natural gas projects, the company says it expects to have seven new projects in 2025. Shares rise 3.1%, to $231.34. (sabela.ojea@wsj.com; @sabelaojeaguix)

1119 ET - DraftKings kicked off the year well with revenue and Ebitda higher than expected in January, Truist analysts say in a research note. Month-to-date in February, the company has seen strong customer acquisitions, retention and engagement, they say. And its sportsbook hold, or share of money made after winning bets are paid out, was 11% in January and 13% in February, against a total of 10.5% for 2024, the analysts say. During the Super Bowl, DraftKings reached the top spot in the app store for sports, saw a record $436 million in bets placed and had a favorable bet mix that generated its best single day yet for gaming revenue from sports-betting, the analysts say. (dean.seal@wsj.com)

1106 ET - With its 4Q report, DraftKings put a bow on a 2024 that saw it clocking huge gains across a variety of metrics, Truist analysts say in a research note. The company acquired 3.5 million new customers at record low acquisition costs and grew its online sports-betting and iGaming handle, or the total amount wagered by bettors, by 21% from the year before, the analysts say. The company's structural hold, or share of money made after paying out winning bets, rose 70 basis points and its promotions rate dropped by 200 basis points, the analysts say. And importantly, 2024 was the company's first year with positive free cash flow, they say. DraftKings jumps 13% to $52.34. (dean.seal@wsj.com)

1038 ET - DraftKings raised its 2025 revenue guidance by $50 million at the midpoint, a lift largely attributed to investments in the company's live betting operations, Benchmark analyst Mike Hickey says in a research note. Part of that investment was its acquisition of Simplebet, which is expected to enhance its live-betting capabilities and product offerings, the analyst says. Simplebet's technology is expected to boost DraftKings's parlay handle mix and structural sportsbook hold percentage, or the cut of money it makes after paying out winning bets, the analyst says. DraftKings surges 14%.(dean.seal@wsj.com)

1033 ET - Starbucks CEO Brian Niccol feels the company's history of resilience will serve it well as it now contends with rapidly changing considerations for tariffs. "As environments change, regulations change, laws change, obviously our job is to figure out how to navigate successfully through all that change," Niccol said in an interview with Bloomberg TV. "I think we've demonstrated over the history of Starbucks, we're pretty resilient when it comes to dealing with change." The coffee chain operates across 88 countries and has a global supply chain behind it, which may help as various components in its products and other goods may soon be subject to higher duties. (denny.jacob@wsj.com; @pennedbyden)

1028 ET - Some economists are taking a glass-half-full look at the disappointing January retail-sales figures from the Commerce Department. Michael Pearce of Oxford Economics argues that poor weather and a less favorable seasonal adjustment were some of the biggest factors behind the 0.9% month-over-month decline. "A resilient labor market, strong household balance sheets, and lower interest rates than a year ago mean we still expect consumer spending to rise at a solid pace in 2025," Pearce writes. Inflation-adjusted consumption could still grow at a 2.6% annualized rate in 1Q, Pearce projects. (matt.grossman@wsj.com; @mattgrossman)

data.title: BADGER METER INC SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Badger Meter Inc., a leading provider of smart water solutions and flow instrumentation, has released its 2024 10-K report, showcasing significant growth in financial performance and strategic advancements. The company, known for its innovative metering solutions, has demonstrated robust growth driven by increased adoption of smart water technologies and strategic acquisitions.

Financial Highlights

Badger Meter Inc. reported impressive financial results for 2024, reflecting strong growth across key metrics:

  • Net Sales: $826.6 million, a 17.5% increase from the previous year, driven by strong growth in smart water solutions and cellular AMI adoption.
  • Gross Margin: $329.2 million, with a gross margin percentage increase from 39.3% in 2023 to 39.8% in 2024, attributed to higher volumes and favorable product and customer sales mix.
  • Operating Earnings: $157.9 million, representing 19.1% of sales, up from 16.8% in 2023, due to increased net sales and improved gross margin.
  • Net Earnings: $124.9 million, an increase from $92.6 million in 2023, driven by higher operating earnings.
  • Diluted Earnings per Share: $4.23, compared to $3.14 in 2023, reflecting the increase in net earnings.

Business Highlights

Badger Meter's business performance in 2024 was marked by significant operational achievements:

  • Revenue Segments: The company's business is divided into two main product lines: Utility Water and Flow Instrumentation. The Utility Water product line, which includes smart metering solutions, accounted for approximately 88% of net sales in 2024. The Flow Instrumentation product line contributed about 12% of net sales.
  • Geographical Performance: North America, primarily the United States, remains the largest geographic market for Badger Meter. The company has also expanded into select regional markets outside the US, such as the Middle East and the UK, with its BlueEdge offering.
  • Sales Units: Strong growth was observed in sales of smart water solutions, particularly in cellular AMI adoption, including mechanical and ultrasonic meters, ORION Cellular endpoints, and BEACON SaaS revenues.
  • New Product Launches: The company continues to innovate with its BlueEdge suite, which includes tailorable solutions for water management technology, software, and support services.
  • Future Outlook: Badger Meter is well-positioned to benefit from ongoing conversion trends to AMI systems and the adoption of smart water solutions. The company is focused on expanding its presence in international markets and enhancing its product offerings to meet increasing customer demands for accurate and actionable data.
  • Operational Efficiency: Improved gross margin as a percentage of sales due to higher volumes and favorable product and customer sales mix. Effective inventory management resulted in a decrease in inventory levels by the end of 2024.
  • Research and Development: Investment of $19.2 million in research and development in 2024, focusing on the development of new products and the improvement of existing products and manufacturing processes.
  • Human Capital Resources: Emphasis on attracting, developing, and retaining talent, with a focus on diversity and inclusion. Various programs to support employee well-being and engagement resulted in a decrease in regrettable turnover to 6.6% in 2024.

Strategic Initiatives

Badger Meter has been actively pursuing strategic initiatives to enhance its market position and product offerings:

  • Strategic Acquisitions: In 2024, the company acquired select remote water monitoring hardware and software, including the Telog product line and Unity Remote Monitoring software, for $3.0 million. In 2023, it acquired Syrinix Ltd., a provider of high-frequency pressure monitoring and leak detection solutions, for $17.1 million. These acquisitions aim to strengthen the company's position in the smart water solutions market.
  • Capital Management: The company maintains a strong financial position with no short-term borrowings as of the end of 2024. It has a $150 million multi-currency line of credit, with $154.1 million of unused credit lines available. Active capital management includes stock repurchases and dividend payments. In February 2023, the board authorized the repurchase of up to 200,000 shares of common stock through February 2026. The company paid dividends of $35.8 million in 2024, reflecting an increase in the dividend per share to $1.22. A stock repurchase of $427,000 was completed in 2022.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to continue its strategic focus on expanding its smart water solutions through acquisitions and organic growth. Anticipated capital expenditures in the range of $15.0-19.0 million for fiscal 2025 will support growth opportunities and asset purchases. The company remains committed to maintaining a strong balance sheet and providing shareholder returns through dividends and share repurchases.

Challenges and Risks

Badger Meter faces several challenges and risks that could impact its business operations and financial performance:

  • Competitive Environment: The company operates in a highly competitive environment with competitors having greater financial resources, which could impact market share and profitability.
  • Supply Chain Risks: Reliance on single suppliers for certain raw materials and components poses a risk of supply chain disruptions, potentially leading to increased costs and delivery delays.
  • Technological Advancements: The inability to develop technologically advanced products could harm future success, as maintaining a competitive advantage requires continued investment in R&D.
  • Product Quality: Failure to manufacture quality products could damage reputation and financial performance. Software products may encounter unexpected defects, affecting operations.
  • Global Risks: Global public health pandemics and geopolitical crises could disrupt business operations.
  • Economic Conditions: Economic conditions, including rising interest rates and government budget cuts, could impact sales.
  • Regulatory Changes: Regulatory changes, including climate change legislation, could increase operational costs.
  • Cybersecurity Threats: Cybersecurity threats and data breaches pose significant risks to operations and reputation.
  • Market Risks: Exposure to market risks such as competition, economic conditions, and foreign currency fluctuations. The company does not typically use derivative instruments, which limits its ability to hedge against certain market risks.

SEC Filing: BADGER METER INC [ BMI ] - 10-K - Feb. 14, 2025

data.title: North Sea Crude-Differentials stable at week's end
data.description:
North Sea Forties and U.S. WTI Midland crude differentials were unchanged on Friday, as offer levels for both grades came in above recent trades.

PLATTS WINDOW

* Indications are on a free-on-board (FOB) basis unless marked as cost, insurance and freight (CIF) or delivered at place (DAP).

* There were no trades on Friday.

* Forties: Chevron bid for a March 8-14 cargo at dated Brent minus 40 cents.

* Forties: TotalEnergies offered a March 6-8 cargo at dated Brent plus 65 cents.

* Forties: Unipec withdrew an offer for February 24-26 at dated Brent minus 15 cents.

* On Wednesday, Glencore bought a cargo from Equinor at dated Brent minus 20 cents.

* WTI Midland: Mercuria withdrew an offer for March 2-6 at dated plus $1.15 CIF Rotterdam.

* On Thursday, Cepsa bought WTI Midland with March 6-10 dates from Gunvor at dated Brent plus $1.20 CIF Rotterdam.

* Also on Thursday, Chevron bought a WTI Midland cargo with February 28-March 4 dates at dated Brent plus 85 cents CIF Rotterdam.

data.title: Diamondback in Talks to Buy Permian Producer Double Eagle — WSJ
data.description:
By Benoît Morenne and Lauren Thomas

Diamondback Energy is in talks to buy Double Eagle, a big West Texas energy producer, a sign that consolidation in the country's biggest oil patch isn't slowing down.

A deal could be announced in coming days, if the talks don't fall apart, according to people familiar with the matter. It's also possible another suitor could emerge.

Double Eagle, backed by private-equity firm EnCap Investments, could be valued at more than $5 billion in the transaction, the people said.

Fort Worth-based Double Eagle owns more than 95,000 net acres in the Midland Basin. The company is one of the last large assets left in the Permian Basin, the largest U.S. oil field.

Diamondback, based in Midland, Texas, has a market value of around $47 billion. The company last year acquired Permian producer Endeavor Energy Resources in a cash-and-stock deal that valued Endeavor at around $26 billion.

Write to Benoît Morenne at benoit.morenne@wsj.com and Lauren Thomas at lauren.thomas@wsj.com

data.title: Dogecoin Adam & Eve Structure Hints At Bullish Potential – Can DOGE Breakout?
data.description:
Dogecoin is trading at critical supply levels as the broader crypto market begins to show signs of life. Over the past few weeks, DOGE has faced significant volatility and uncertainty, with the price dropping to lower demand zones. This period of turbulence has left analysts divided on its next move. Some predict a bearish continuation, while others anticipate a swift reversal driven by renewed market interest.

Top analyst Bluntz has weighed in on Dogecoin’s price action, sharing a technical analysis that suggests a potential bullish setup. According to Bluntz, DOGE is forming an Adam & Eve bullish pattern on the 4-hour time frame. This pattern, characterized by a sharp “V” bottom followed by a rounded “U” bottom, is often a precursor to a breakout to higher levels if confirmed.

This technical structure has given hope to investors who expect DOGE to recover and potentially lead the meme coin market higher. However, much will depend on whether Dogecoin can hold key levels and gain the momentum needed for a breakout. As the market continues to stir from its recent slumber, DOGE’s price action could provide critical insights into the next trend for the crypto space.

Dogecoin Price Hints At Recovery

Dogecoin is currently trading at key supply levels, attempting to reclaim critical price points to confirm the start of a recovery rally. Analysts are increasingly optimistic, calling for a potential breakout as the intense fear that gripped the market earlier in February begins to fade. Dogecoin, often considered the leader of the meme coin sector, is in the spotlight, with investors closely watching its next move.

The meme coin market, which has faced significant hate and criticism in recent months due to aggressive selloffs, now sees Dogecoin as a potential driver of a healthier phase. A DOGE recovery could signal renewed optimism and set the tone for other meme coins to follow.

Top crypto analyst Bluntz shared an encouraging technical analysis on X, highlighting a bullish setup for Dogecoin. Bluntz stated, “Lots of nice Adam and Eve structures across the board are starting to break out here. Nice on DOGE, as it’s been nearly 2 weeks since the capitulation wick.” This observation refers to a classic bullish pattern, where a sharp “V” bottom is followed by a rounded “U” bottom, often signaling the potential for an upward breakout.

Dogecoin’s ability to reclaim critical price levels and hold above them will be pivotal in confirming a sustained recovery rally. Should the bullish momentum continue, DOGE could lead the meme coin sector back into focus, restoring investor confidence in this unique niche of the crypto market. The coming weeks will be crucial in determining whether Dogecoin can establish itself as a market leader once again and drive a broader recovery across the sector.

DOGE Testing Crucial Supply

Dogecoin is trading at $0.27 after several days of volatility and sideways trading. The price has been ranging between $0.23 and $0.27, reflecting the uncertainty that has gripped the market in recent weeks. However, bulls are beginning to show signs of life, pushing DOGE toward key supply levels and signaling potential momentum for a breakout.

If DOGE can reclaim the $0.305 mark in the coming days, it could pave the way for a massive recovery rally. This level is a crucial supply zone, and flipping it into support would provide a strong confirmation of a trend reversal, reigniting bullish sentiment across the meme coin market.

On the flip side, if DOGE loses support at the $0.23 level, it risks falling into deeper correction territory. Such a move would likely signal that selling pressure is overwhelming buyer demand, potentially driving the price to test lower demand zones.

For now, all eyes are on whether Dogecoin can break out of its current range and establish a decisive direction. The coming days will be critical in determining whether DOGE can maintain its upward momentum or face further downside as market participants look for clear signals of recovery or continued consolidation.

Featured image from Dall-E, chart from TradingView

data.title: US gives conditional nod to Zoetis' bird flu vaccine for poultry
data.description:
The U.S. Department of Agriculture gave Zoetis conditional approval to use its bird flu vaccine in poultry, the animal healthcare company said on Friday.

A conditional approval, called as a conditional license, is used for emergencies, limited market availability, or other special circumstance and is issued for a finite period of time.

Bird flu has infected nearly 70 people in the United States, with one death, since last April. Most of those infections have been among farm workers exposed to infected poultry or cows.

Zoetis, which deals with vaccines, medicines as well as diagnostic solutions for animals, said the conditional license was granted based on reasonable expectation of efficacy.

data.title: SoundHound's stock sinks as Nvidia sells off stake. What comes next?
data.description:
By Emily Bary

Nvidia's latest 13-F filing also indicates it sold its stake in Serve Robotics, and shares of the self-driving-technology company are also sliding

Many investors hadn't heard of SoundHound AI Inc. before a year ago, when Nvidia Corp. filed its first 13-F form, which showed a position in the maker of voice-based artificial-intelligence technology.

SoundHound shares (SOUN) surged some 570% in the year through Thursday's close, as investors took a closer look at the company's potential to win more AI partnerships.

From the archives (February 2024): Nvidia discloses positions in SoundHound AI, Arm - and these other stocks

But shares were falling 28% in Friday morning action after Nvidia's (NVDA) latest 13-F no longer showed a position in SoundHound as of the end of the December quarter.

Nvidia declined to comment on the filing. SoundHound didn't immediately respond to MarketWatch's request for comment.

Quarterly 13-F forms are required for investors that "exercise investment discretion over $100 million," according to the Securities and Exchange Commission. While these forms typically come from professional investment managers, they can also come from companies that hold significant investments in public companies.

Nvidia owned 1.7 million shares of SoundHound, valued at just over $8 million, as of its September 13-F.

Though SoundHound's stock is down following the latest filing, the company could continue to capture investor attention even without the Nvidia tie. SoundHound is expected to post $84 million in revenue for 2024 but was valued at $5.6 billion prior to Friday's selloff, suggesting the stock has become a broader speculative bet on future AI winners.

Wedbush analyst Daniel Ives called SoundHound "a long-term winner in the AI revolution" in a note to clients last December.

See also: SoundHound diversifies its business beyond cars, but the hot AI stock retreats

Nvidia also owned 3.7 million shares of Serve Robotics Inc. (SERV), a maker of self-driving delivery technology. The shares were valued at $29.6 million at the end of the September quarter. That position is gone as well from Nvidia's latest 13-F for December.

Serve Robotics shares were taking an even steeper Friday morning dive, off 44%. Nvidia's Serve position represented 8.4% of the shares outstanding, and Nvidia was the second-largest shareholder, behind Uber Technologies Inc. (UBER), according to FactSet data.

Serve Robotics didn't immediately respond to a MarketWatch request for comment.

Nvidia's latest filing also shows a pruning of its stake in Arm Holdings PLC (ARM), the hot chip-design stock. Nvidia owned 1.1 million shares as of Dec. 31, whereas it owned nearly 2 million shares as of Sept. 30.

Arm's stock is down 4.5% in Friday morning action.

The 13-F newly shows stakes in WeRide Inc. (WRD), a Chinese autonomous-driving company, and Nebius Group N.V. (NBIS), an AI-infrastructure company. However, 13-F filings are not always clear indicators of recent investment activity, as they can also reflect a requirement to disclose prior investments in companies that have gone public during the reporting period. For instance, WeRide went public during the December quarter and said in its prospectus that Nvidia became an "early-stage investor" in 2017. Nebius saw its trading resume in October after it had been suspended in 2022 related to its ownership of Russian assets that have since been divested.

Still, Nebius shares are up 9% in morning trading and WeRide shares are nearly doubling.

The overall value of Nvidia's holdings dropped to $304.5 million from $432.9 million the previous quarter.

-Emily Bary

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Freedom Holdings, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Freedom Holdings, Inc., a company primarily engaged in the renewable energy sector, has released its Form 10-Q report for the first quarter of 2025. The report showcases significant financial and operational achievements, driven by strategic acquisitions and a focus on sustainable energy development.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenues: $14.79 million, driven by the acquisition of TAG and primarily related to solar installation projects.

Gross Margin: $7.54 million, reflecting the difference between revenues and costs of revenues.

Net Income (Loss) from Operations: $7.31 million, indicating strong operational performance following the TAG acquisition.

Net Income (Loss) Prior to Income Taxes: $7.29 million, showcasing a significant turnaround from the previous year's loss.

Basic and Diluted Income (Loss) Per Share: $0.12, highlighting improved profitability per share compared to the previous period.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The company generated revenues primarily from solar installation projects, which were directly related to the acquisition of The Awareness Group (TAG) in September 2024.

Operational Expansion: The acquisition of TAG has significantly contributed to the company's operations, leading to increased revenues and operational activities in the solar installation sector.

Future Outlook: The company plans to continue leveraging the TAG acquisition to enhance its capabilities in renewable energy, with a focus on sustainable energy development.

New Acquisition: On January 25, 2025, the company acquired a 51% majority ownership in Renewable Energy Products Manufacturing Corp. (REPM), which is expected to integrate into the TAG GRID and enhance renewable energy capabilities.

Operational Challenges: The company faces uncertainties such as economic changes, governmental restrictions, and the need for working capital to support project development and expansion plans.

Management's Focus: The management is focused on generating sufficient cash flows from operations and securing additional funding to support the company's business plan and future growth initiatives.

SEC Filing: Freedom Holdings, Inc. [ FHLD ] - 10-Q - Feb. 14, 2025

data.title: U.S. Oil Rig Count Rises by 1 to 481 — Market Talk
data.description: The number of rigs drilling for oil in the U.S. edged up by one this week to 481, which was 16 fewer than a year ago, oil services company Baker Hughes reports, while natural gas rigs increased by one to 101, down 20 from a year before. In the Dallas Fed's 4Q energy survey published last month, two-thirds of exploration and production executives who anticipate increasing investment this year beyond their previous estimate said they expect to invest more in drilling and completion of wells. A majority of executives also expect permitting times for drilling wells on federal lands to decrease over the next four years. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)
